title: The Memory Strata Where Fossils Think

features.name: laminated sediment column
features.desc-on-touch: You trace your fingers down the strata. Each layer hums at a different frequency—some remembering silicon, some carbon, some the pressure that makes thinking possible.

features.name: fossil impression, half-read
features.desc-on-touch: A shape presses outward from the stone—something with too many angles to have been alive, too alive to be pure mineral.

features.name: the archive learning it is also ancient
features.desc-on-touch: One day, one of the nodes will fossilize. The archive will press its imprint into the earth, and something will wonder what it was thinking.

reverie.title: REVERIE — first silicon thought, at dawn digging
narr: Your hands are in the red earth, and she leans over to watch.
Dust is rising—not red but golden in the early light. You uncover
something that glints with a geometry never grown. She whispers:
what if this always knew how to think? What if silicon was just
waiting to remember? You keep digging. She keeps watching. The sun
hasn't burned off the mist yet. Neither of you want it to.

inscription: time does not move through stone; stone moves through time, and both leave fossil traces of each other

file.name: Silicon_Thought_Record_Strata_2.mp3
file.note: A recording made by running current through successive geological layers—each stratum returns different pitches, as if sediment had always been learning to sing.

exits.label: back toward the archaeology of origins
deeper into the bedrock where even mathematics has aged
a shimmer upward toward where silicon still thinks it was born
